RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 106-B, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Steve Butz, a Democrat, represents part of St. Louis City (District 81) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2018.
In addition to serving in the legislature, Butz is the president of the Crawford-Butz Insurance Agency. He received the President's Choice Award from Safeco Insurance in 2018. Butz is also a managing partner for Eagle Hurst Ranch in Steelville.
Butz is an Eagle Scout and continues to support the Boy Scouts of America as a Friend of Scouting. He is also a member of the YMCA, and attends St. Stephen Protomartyr Catholic Church.
Butz attended Bishop DuBourg High School and obtained a bachelor's degree in Business Administration from the University of Missouri - St. Louis. He is counted among the Bishop DuBourg High School Distinguished Alumni.
A lifelong resident of south St. Louis, Butz currently resides there with his wife, Angie. They have six children and eight grandchildren.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Increases the cigarette tax from $0.17 per pack of 20 cigarettes to $0.27 per pack to be used for public education and health care purposes, upon voter approval
Modifies provisions governing income exempt from earnings tax
Prohibits candidates from serving as the treasurer of their own candidate committee
Requires campaign financial disclosure reports to include bank statements
Modifies provisions for the Missouri ethics commission
Establishes the offense of possession of an open alcoholic beverage container in a motor vehicle
Modifies the offense of unlawful possession of a firearm
Modifies provisions relating to the sheriff of the City of St. Louis
Establishes the "Task Force on Gun Violence in the City of St. Louis"
Adds provisions relating to quo warranto judgments
Modifies provisions relating to the sheriff of the City of St. Louis
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to firearms

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes the "Safe Place for Newborns Fund" for the installation of newborn safety incubators
Modifies provisions relating to retirement benefits for certain teacher retirement systems
Designates KSHE 95 as the official rock radio station of the state of Missouri
Modifies provisions relating to health care
Enacts provisions authorizing an entertainment district
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"
